examples.title: Dynamic Array Implementation
examples.description: Complete dynamic array data structure from scratch

examples.title: Insert and Delete Operations
examples.description: Insert at arbitrary position and delete operations

languages.python:
# Python list IS a dynamic array
arr = []

# Common operations
arr.append(10)          # O(1) amortized
arr.append(20)
arr.insert(1, 15)       # O(n) - insert at index
arr.pop()               # O(1) - remove last
arr.pop(0)              # O(n) - remove first
val = arr[0]            # O(1) - access
arr[0] = 5              # O(1) - update
len(arr)                # O(1) - size

# Pre-allocation for known size
arr = [0] * 1000        # Create with 1000 zeros
arr = [None] * 1000     # Create with 1000 None values

# List comprehension
arr = [i**2 for i in range(10)]  # [0, 1, 4, 9, 16, ...]

# Python equivalent operations to C++ vector

# Declaration equivalents
v = []                    # Empty
v = [0] * 10              # 10 zeros
v = [5] * 10              # 10 fives
v = [1, 2, 3]             # Initialized

# Operations
v.append(10)              # push_back
v.pop()                   # pop_back
v[0] = 5                  # Access/modify
v[0]                      # front
v[-1]                     # back
len(v)                    # size
# No direct capacity equivalent
not v                     # empty check

# Iteration
for x in v:
    print(x)

for i, x in enumerate(v):
    print(f"v[{i}] = {x}")
